NARRATIVE:
EXAM: Chest 2 Views, 07/12/17.
 
HISTORY: 57 years Male, Reassess pleural effusion.
 
COMPARISON: 17/7/12.
 
FINDINGS:
 
There is a pleural catheter in the inferior right lung, stable in 
position compared to the prior study.  There has been resolution of 
the previous subcutaneous emphysema over the right chest.  There is a 
moderate sized right pleural effusion which has a loculated component 
extending to the right apex.  There is no pneumothorax.  Overall the 
pleural effusion is similar in size compared to the prior study.  The 
left lung is clear.  There are posttraumatic changes of the left 
clavicle..

COMPARED TO THE PRIOR STUDY.

report:
NARRATIVE:
PORTABLE CHEST, SINGLE VIEW: 1/14/2005.
CLINICAL INFORMATION:
Patient with lymphoma. Intensive Care Unit admission. Please
evaluate.
COMPARISON:
1-14-2005.
FINDINGS:
There has been interval removal of the right subclavian catheter.
An endotracheal tube is present with the tip several centimeters
above the carina. Interval decrease in lung volumes. Interval
development of bilateral pleural effusions and reticular opacities
in both lungs. Changes are consistent with pulmonary edema.
Decreased aeration in both bases with bibasilar opacities. This
may represent atelectasis and/or pneumonia. There is no evidence
of pneumothorax.
IMPRESSION:
1. ENDOTRACHEAL TUBE PRESENT WITH TIP SEVERAL CENTIMETERS ABOVE
THE CARINA.
2. LOW LUNG VOLUMES.
3. MILD PULMONARY EDEMA.
4. BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS.
5. BIBASILAR OPACITIES THAT MAY REPRESENT ATELECTASIS AND/OR
PNEUMONIA.
END OF IMPRESSION.

ACTION.

with the report transcribed above.
